revision.text:

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Udea}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Ikushimatarushima-jinja keidai.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36.36025|N|138.218194|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_caption = Ikushimatarushima Shrine in Japan | location = {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|qid=Q844852|lt=Ueda|1=Ueda, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} | website = {{official|https://oki-dougo.info/data01/room/sroom/shrine/sroom_mizuwakasu.html}} | name = Ikushima Tarushima Shrine | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|qid=Q844852|lt=Ueda|1=Ueda, Nagano}}, {{ill|Shimonogo, Ueda|ja|下之郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonogo, Ueda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Nakai Nishi 701 | deities = {{ubl|{{ill|Ikushima Ōkami|ja|生島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|{{ill|Tarushima Ōkami|ja|足島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} | sacred_object = The earthen floor in the inner sanctum | shrine_Rank = {{ubl|{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 2 seats)|Former {{ill|National Shrine|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}|{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}}}} | founded = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | nicknames = | pilgrimage = | festival = September 19 | rituals = {{ubl|{{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|qid=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira Festival|1=Onbashira}} (every six years)|{{ill|Okoromi-sai|ja|御籠祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okoromi-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November 3 - April 13)}} | map = {{location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=21|lat_sec=36.9|lon_deg=138|lon_min=13|lon_sec=5.5|label=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|zoom=11|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|21|36.9|N|138|13|5.5|E}}|type=point}} | prefecture= {{ill|Ueda|qid=Q844852|1=Ueda, Nagano}} | architecture_style= {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} }} {{coord list}}
'''Ikushimatarushima Shrine''', also known as '''Ikushima Tarushima Shrine''', is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|qid=Q844852|lt=Ueda|1=Ueda, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=iahLAQAAIAAJ&q=Ikushimatarushima+Shrine |title=Musashino |date=1915 |publisher=Japan Office |language=en}}</ref> It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it. It is also a [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] recorded as a highly Ranked Shrine in the [[Engishiki]] in 927.
It enshrines [[Ikushima Okami]] and [[Tarushima Okami]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-06-22 |title=Ikushimatarushima Shrine {{!}} Ueda Official Tourism Website |url=https://go.ueda-kanko.or.jp/spot/ikushimatarushima-shrine/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=Ueda Official Tourism Website {{!}} The official website of the Ueda Tourist Association of Shinshu, Nagano Prefecture. The site provides information on sightseeing spots including Ueda Castle, accommodations, and local gourmet foods, as well as information on conventions and educational tours. |language=en}}</ref>
The Ikushimatarushima Shrine is home to a series of allegiance pledges given to Shingen by over a hundred retainers, originating from the years 1566 and 1567.<ref>{{Cite book |last1=Castiglioni |first1=Andrea |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=1rsEEAAAQBAJ&dq=Ikushimatarushima+Shrine&pg=PA253 |title=Defining Shugendo: Critical Studies on Japanese Mountain Religion |last2=Rambelli |first2=Fabio |last3=Roth |first3=Carina |date=2020-11-12 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing |isbn=978-1-350-17940-0 |language=en}}</ref>
It is described as a Ubusuna-jinja or one with a very local focus.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book |last=Herbert |first=Jean |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |date=2010-09-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-415-59348-9 |edition=1st |location=London |language=English}}</ref>{{Rp|page=346}}
Its name comes from two deities: Ikushima-no-kami; and Tarushima-no-kami.<ref name=":0" />
==Overview== [[File:Ikushimatarushimajinja.png|thumb|{{center|Crest of Ikushima Tarushima Shrine}}]] [[File:Ikushimatarushima-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|{{center|East Torii}}]] '''Ikushima Tarushima Shrine''' ({{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|qid=Q11574513|lt=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|1=Ikushimatarushima Shrine}}, ''Ikushimatarushima Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|qid=Q844852|lt=Ueda|1=Ueda, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and a former {{ill|National Shrine|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, currently listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines two main deities: * '''{{ill|Ikushima Ōkami|ja|生島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – The god of creation, fostering all life and vitality. * '''{{ill|Tarushima Ōkami|ja|足島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarushima Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – The god of fulfillment and prosperity across the land.
The ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' (Register of Deities in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}) mentions both deities as being enshrined in the Imperial Court's Department of Divinities (Jingikan), indicating their importance to the Imperial family.<ref name="宮中">Refer to {{ill|List of shrines in the Imperial Court and capital|ja|宮中・京中の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in the Imperial Court and capital|1=List of Shikinaisha in the Imperial Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453224}}.</ref>
== History == === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is uncertain. According to legend, {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} (deity of the auxiliary {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}) stopped here during his journey to Suwa and offered rice porridge to Ikushima and Tarushima.<ref name="由緒書">Shrine's official history.</ref> This establishes Ikushima and Tarushima as local deities. The tradition is preserved in the unique ''Okoromi-sai'' (Retreating Festival).
Some theories propose that the shrine was founded by the {{ill|Kina-no-Kuni clan|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kina-no-Kuni clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, to honor their ancestral gods brought from the Imperial Court.<ref name="平凡">{{Cite book|editor=Heibonsha Local Data Center|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}}|volume=Nagano Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1979|chapter=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|isbn=978-4582490206}}</ref>
=== Development === During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the ''Shinshō Chokufu Shō'' mentions that the shrine received one household from {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} for its upkeep. By the {{ill|Engi era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97379316}} (927), it was classified as a ''Myōjin Taisha'' in the ''Engishiki''.
During the medieval era, the shrine enjoyed patronage from prominent figures like {{ill|Hōjō Tokimune|ja|北条国時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō Tokimune|1=Hōjō Kunitoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11402090}} and the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|qid=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}. [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dedicated prayers for success against {{ill|Uesugi Kenshin|en|Uesugi Kenshin|qid=Q311080|lt=Uesugi Kenshin|1=Uesugi Kenshin}}, leaving behind sacred documents that are now Important Cultural Properties.
After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was Ranked a ''Kensha'' in 1871 and promoted to ''Kokuhē Chūsha'' in 1899.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Official Gazette|issue=4814|date=1899-07-19}}</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine is situated on a small island in a pond, representing the ancient ''Miya-no-miya'' style.<ref name="平凡" /> Its main hall, built in 1941, faces north towards the auxiliary {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}. The two shrines are connected by the sacred ''Shinbashi Bridge,'' used only during specific rituals.
The ''Inner Shrine'' (''Naiden'') inside the main hall was formerly the original hall. Built during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, it is a designated Nagano Prefectural Treasure.<ref name="内殿">{{Cite web|url=http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4386|title=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine Inner Shrine|publisher=Nagano Prefecture Cultural Properties}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * ''Ikushima Tarushima Shrine Documents'' – 94 scrolls, including pledges signed by the Takeda clan vassals.
=== Prefectural Cultural Properties === * ''Inner Shrine'' – Designated Nagano Prefectural Treasure. * ''Kabuki Stage'' – The largest rural kabuki stage in Japan.<ref name="舞台">{{Cite web|url=http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2937|title=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine Kabuki Stage|publisher=Nagano Prefecture Cultural Properties}}</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|Ueda Dentetsu|ja|上田電鉄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Dentetsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上田交通|1=Ueda Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11263296}}, {{ill|Bessho Line|ja|別所線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bessho Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上田電鉄別所線|1=Ueda Electric Railway Bessho Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7877419}}, {{ill|Shimonogō Station|en|Shimonogō Station|qid=Q11360592|lt=Shimonogo Station|1=Shimonogō Station}}, 5 minutes on foot. * Bus: {{ill|Chikuma Bus|ja|千曲バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Bus|1=Chikuma Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11405568}}, ''Ikushima Tarushima Shrine Stop.''
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.ikushimatarushima.com/ Ikushima Tarushima Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/03tousin/01jousyou/ikushimatarushima.html Ikushima Tarushima Shrine – Nagano Prefecture Shrine Office]
